mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remaining near the zero-to-low bound (below 0.5%) signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into risk assets.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.34 as of t-1mo, near historic lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of FEDFUNDS<0.5% show no statistically significant positive mean return versus periods above 0.5% (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces non-negative mean 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in both RSAFS (retail sales) and INDPRO (industrial production) indicates synchronized demand-side and supply-side expansion that reinforces corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone reliably predicts the move.
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS momentum x INDPRO momentum) fails to outperform either single-series model on out-of-sample Rank IC by more than 0.02, or joint-acceleration periods show negative mean forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST (housing starts) during a late-cycle expansion regime signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and foreshadows broader economic slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; this relationship is weak or absent in early-cycle or recovery regimes.
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle windows, HOUST decline periods show non-negative mean 60-day forward returns, or the effect disappears once controlling for regime classification (interaction with cycle-stage dummy insignificant, p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month growth in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects sustained labor demand and consumer income growth, supporting near-term equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth months fail to show statistically significant higher mean 5-day forward returns than negative growth months (t-test p>0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above 2.5%, markets price in higher probability of Federal Reserve tightening, raising the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation periods show mean 20-day forward returns not significantly different from baseline (p>0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of low FEDFUNDS (below 1%) combined with elevated UNRATE (above 4.5%) reflects a policy environment where the Fed maintains accommodation despite labor slack, reinforcing a 'lower for longer' expectation that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the effect requires both conditions jointly, as either alone is ambiguous.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.34, UNRATE=4.9 as of decision date
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with labor slack
failure_condition: Joint-condition periods produce mean 60-day forward returns not significantly greater than unconditional baseline (p>0.05), or the interaction term's marginal Rank IC contribution over single-factor models is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in GDPC1 quarterly growth rate below 2% annualized during a late-cycle expansion regime signals diminishing aggregate demand momentum, leading investors to discount future earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in early-cycle recoveries the same deceleration is not predictive because base effects dominate.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth deceleration below 2% annualized in late-cycle windows fails to produce mean negative 60-day forward returns, or the regime interaction (cycle-stage x GDPC1 growth) is statistically insignificant (p>0.10).
